constraints.rebalance_negative_test_invariant: ntm rebalance must run dry-run only; --apply is not authorized by W4T
constraints.ensemble_negative_test_invariant: ntm ensemble export-findings can route findings to beads ONLY after Phase 4 dispatch authorization; this triage produces recommendations only
constraints.add_negative_test_invariant: ntm add candidate is classified no_fit because no W3 audit cited a missing native primitive that requires it

candidates.rationale: Native ntm rebalance ships --dry-run / --apply discipline, --format json, and a workload-imbalance score that maps cleanly to a flywheel gate. Live probe shows imbalance_score=0 and recommendation=balanced, so there is no current need to apply. The fit is real but the operational hook should be advisory until W3a coordinator-shadow evidence shows imbalance signal that auto-refill-decision-log.sh cannot already reproduce.
candidates.wrapper_delta: no wrapper authored in W4T (dry-run only). A future Phase 4 bead may add a thin wrapper that consumes ntm rebalance --format json and routes the recommendation into the existing auto-refill-decision-log.sh feed without replacing existing pane workload signals.
candidates.ttl_native: command-bound (rebalance is a one-shot analysis, no lease)
candidates.ttl_decision: preserve native one-shot semantics; wrapper would only add evidence rows, not extend command lifetime
candidates.native_wrapper_delta: native ntm rebalance owns workload-imbalance scoring; wrapper would own evidence-row write to dispatch-log.jsonl when imbalance crosses configured threshold
forbidden_operations: ntm rebalance <session> --apply (no W4T authority for runtime state mutation)
any rebalance call without --dry-run during this triage
evidence: live ntm rebalance flywheel --format json --dry-run probe at 2026-05-09T15:38:51Z returned imbalance_score=0
plan section 6 row W4 example: 'rebalance/ensemble run dry-run over W1-W3 receipts and produce recommendations only'
plan section 7 row W4T: dry-run only; no new beads until Phase 4 dispatch explicitly authorizes
plan section 9 risk row 'W4 triage expands into implementation' (severity=med) mitigated by dry-run only
candidates.phase_4_followup_summary: Wrapper bead to thread rebalance recommendation into auto-refill-decision-log.sh feed; reservation targets and acceptance gates to be authored in a separate Phase 4 dispatch.

candidates.rationale: Native ntm ensemble ships 9 embedded presets, deterministic checkpointing, and an export-findings primitive that converts ensemble outputs into beads. The export-findings primitive is the high-leverage NTM-unaware callsite candidate: today the flywheel uses ad-hoc bead authoring; routing through ntm ensemble export-findings would pin findings to versioned ensemble runs with provenance.
candidates.wrapper_delta: no wrapper authored in W4T (dry-run only). A future Phase 4 bead may add a thin wrapper that runs ntm ensemble <preset> on a flywheel learning topic and pipes export-findings through L52 bead-receipt discipline. The wrapper's job is to preserve flywheel idempotency_token and citation requirements (L56 ladder) when ensemble findings are auto-converted to beads.
candidates.ttl_native: ensemble run TTL = run-bound; cache TTL is configurable
candidates.ttl_decision: native run TTL governs ensemble lifetime; wrapper TTL is irrelevant until the wrapper exists
candidates.native_wrapper_delta: native ntm ensemble owns reasoning runs, presets, checkpoint, provenance, and finding export; wrapper would own L52 bead-citation enforcement and idempotency_token preservation when findings are converted to beads
authorized_operations: ntm ensemble presets (read-only listing of available presets)
forbidden_operations: ntm ensemble export-findings (would create beads; W4T is dry-run only)
ntm ensemble spawn (would mutate session state; not authorized by W4T)
any ensemble run that materially mutates .beads/issues.jsonl during this triage
evidence: ntm ensemble --help (probed 2026-05-09T15:38Z; lists subcommands cache, clean-checkpoints, compare, estimate, export, export-findings, import, presets, provenance, rerun-mode, spawn)
live ntm ensemble presets at 2026-05-09T15:38:51Z returned 9 presets across analysis/creative/consensus/adversarial/deliberative strategies
plan section 6 row W4 example: 'rebalance/ensemble run dry-run over W1-W3 receipts and produce recommendations only'
plan section 7 row W4T: dry-run only; no new beads until Phase 4 dispatch explicitly authorizes
candidates.phase_4_followup_summary: Wrapper bead to integrate ntm ensemble export-findings with L52 bead-citation discipline and L56 ladder citation enforcement; reservation targets and acceptance gates to be authored in a separate Phase 4 dispatch.

candidates.rationale: Per plan section 11 row 'add had low but non-zero scores: Final no-fit', plan section 7 row W4T 'no new beads until Phase 4 dispatch explicitly authorizes', and plan section 9 risk row 'W4 triage expands into implementation' (severity=med). No W3 audit cited a missing native primitive that requires ntm add; the existing fleet-spawn discipline (canonical pane shape: pane 1 claude orch + panes 2/3/4 codex per memory rule feedback_canonical_ntm_spawn_shape) covers fleet bring-up without ad-hoc agent additions to live sessions. ntm add as a runtime primitive risks shape drift away from the locked canonical fleet shape.
candidates.wrapper_delta: no wrapper authored; no wrapper recommended. A future audit MAY revisit this if a bead surfaces a concrete missing primitive (e.g., a need to add a labeled review-only agent to an active session that the canonical shape does not cover). Until then, ntm add is excluded from the migration scope.
candidates.native_wrapper_delta: no migration; ntm add remains a native NTM surface available to operators outside flywheel automation
forbidden_operations: ntm add invoked from any flywheel automation script during this migration
evidence: plan section 11 disagreement-resolution row: 'add had low but non-zero scores. Final: no-fit. It stays out of implementation and only appears in W4 triage as an excluded candidate.'
plan section 7 row W4T: dry-run only; no new beads until Phase 4 dispatch explicitly authorizes
plan section 9 risk row 'W4 triage expands into implementation' (severity=med) mitigated by classifying add as no_fit
memory rule feedback_canonical_ntm_spawn_shape: canonical fleet shape (pane 1 claude orch + panes 2/3/4 codex) is locked; ntm add risks shape drift
candidates.phase_4_followup_summary: no_fit; future audit may revisit if a concrete missing primitive surfaces. No Phase 4 bead recommended.

negative_invariants_checked.add_classified_no_fit_unless_bead_cites_: PASS — no bead in the W1-W3 closed dependency set (14 closed deps) cites a missing native primitive that ntm add solves; classification is no_fit
negative_invariants_checked.no_runtime_state_changes: PASS — only the recommendations file is written; .beads/issues.jsonl is reservation-locked but not mutated
negative_invariants_checked.no_new_beads_filed: PASS — beads_filed=0; phase_4_followup_required flagged for rebalance and ensemble but no bead authored at this stage

rollback.side_effects: none beyond removing this report; .beads/issues.jsonl unchanged; cross-orch-coordination.jsonl unchanged

ttls.ttl_native: command-bound per candidate; rebalance and add are one-shot, ensemble runs have configurable cache TTL
ttls.ttl_decision: preserve native command-bound TTLs; wrappers (if authored in Phase 4) own evidence-row TTL only, not command lifetime

native_wrapper_delta_summary: W4T authored zero wrappers. Native ntm rebalance and ntm ensemble are recommended as fit candidates with Phase 4 follow-up beads to add wrappers that thread their JSON output into existing flywheel evidence channels (auto-refill-decision-log.sh, L52 bead-citation enforcement). ntm add is excluded from wrapper scope.
secret_scan_method: no secret-class fields in this report; only public command surfaces, file paths, plan citations, and live probe results (imbalance_score, preset names) are recorded
